codeReview.summary: The feature adds a dedicated must-change-password corridor filter after tenant binding, which keeps the allowed auth corridor intentionally narrow while preserving company-context enforcement ahead of the corridor decision. The accompanying JWT and token-revocation precision changes address same-second relogin edge cases rather than widening session scope, and the updated integration plus runtime coverage exercises both denied protected work and the allowed password-change/logout/refresh/me/profile paths.

summary: PASS. Must-change-password sessions are confined to the intended corridor, tenant binding remains in force, and the millisecond-issued-at revocation path closes the immediate-relogin edge case without a blocking contract regression.
